In this task, you are given a text from tweets and a boolean question whether this tweet has positive sentiment or negative sentiment. Your task is to generate answer "yes" when the tweet has that particular sentiment, otherwise generate answer "no".

PARAGRAPH 1  
The task requires decomposing a complex system prompt into four independent modules, each addressing a distinct aspect of the task. Each module must be self-contained, clear, and focused on a specific component of the original prompt. The goal is to ensure that the final response is complete, accurate, and meets all specified constraints.

PARAGRAPH 1  
The response must be in English. Use proper punctuation and avoid markdown. All words with all capital letters should appear less than three times. The response must start and end with the same word. The total number of sentences must be less than 36. Avoid the use of periods (i.e., dots) as punctuation. The response must be complete and meet all the given constraints.

PARAGRAPH 1  
The final answer must be "yes" or "no" based on the sentiment of the tweet. The answer should be generated in a single section, with no additional text or explanation.

PARAGRAPH 1  
The answer must be "yes" if the tweet has positive sentiment, otherwise "no". The response must be concise and meet all the constraints specified in the task. The answer must be in the same word at the start and end of the response.
